reports.description: Raised mud embankment remains passable for walking evacuation toward camp gate. Not suitable for vehicles. Route should be marked with ropes or flags before sunset to prevent slips during low visibility.

Eight children and two caretakers moved to roof after boundary wall collapsed. Heavy rain continues and phone battery is below 10 percent. Nearby residents can signal with flashlights but cannot cross due to waist-deep flow.

reports.reportName: Boat Fuel Shortage In Rescue Sector
reports.description: Rescue team has boats available but fuel left for only one additional trip. At least 27 residents still waiting on elevated homes. Request urgent diesel cans plus one paramedic team due to reported fever cases among evacuees.

reports.reportName: Community Kitchen Ready With Dry Rations
reports.description: Local committee has prepared rice, dal, biscuits, and ORS packs. Potable water tanker arrived at 06:20. Capacity around 300 meal portions this cycle, with preference for children and elders first.

Two-wheelers are getting swept sideways near the eastern approach. Water depth estimated at 2.5 feet and still rising. Recommend immediate barricading and reroute via higher service road before dawn traffic starts.

reports.reportName: School Campus Confirmed As Safe Zone
reports.description: Ground is dry, electricity is stable, and hand-pump water is available. Community members have already arranged basic mats and lanterns. Space is sufficient for approximately 150 people for 24 hours.

Local volunteer team found a 62-year-old dialysis patient who missed treatment due to road closure. Current medicines will run out by tonight. The family requests transport support to nearest functioning unit and immediate supply of BP and diabetes medication.

Water entered ground floor after midnight and rose to chest level within 40 minutes. A family of five, including a pregnant woman, is now on the terrace with one torch and no food. Neighbors report strong current in the lane, so boat extraction is required rather than on-foot evacuation.
Multiple people stranded near broken bridge section
Child and elderly person stranded on rooftop
Two injured seniors need immediate transport
Cooked meals and drinking water available for 300
Dry elevated area can hold 120 people
Family of 4 trapped on 2nd floor, water rising quickly
